12th thoracic vertebra.
\n2. New 0.7 cm focus of arterial phase hyperenhancement in segment IVb, without washout or impeded diffusion, LR-3.
\n3. No new lesions suspicious for hepatocellular carcinoma.

03/01/2024

\n

[IV CONTRAST]

[ORAL CONTRAST]

[FIELD STRENGTH]

[3D RECONSTRUCTIONS]

\n

[FINDINGS]
FINDINGS:
FINDINGS:
\nLIVER: Operative changes of segment 5/6 resection followed by Y90 treatment to the right lobe. Progressively atrophic posterior right hepatic lobe and hypertrophic left lobe. No new lesions suspicious for hepatocellular carcinoma.
\nGALLBLADDER: Absent. BILIARY: No ductal dilation or filling defect. PANCREAS: No solid mass or ductal dilation. SPLEEN: No splenomegaly.
\nADRENAL GLANDS: Unchanged lipid rich nodular thickening of the left adrenal gland. Normal right adrenal gland. KIDNEYS: No renal collecting system dilation or solid mass. Subcentimeter bilateral renal cysts. Mild wall thickening and enhancement of the left renal pelvis.
\nBOWEL: No abnormal bowel thickening or dilatation.
\nPERITONEUM/RETROPERITONEUM: Trace perihepatic fluid. LYMPH NODES: Borderline enlarged porta hepatis lymph nodes up to 1.1 cm, probably reactive. VESSELS: Normal caliber abdominal aorta with atherosclerotic calcifications. Patent left portal vein. Diminutive but patent right anterior and posterior portal veins. Patent right middle and left hepatic, splenic and superior mesenteric veins.
\nPELVIC ORGANS: No pelvic mass.
\nBONES AND SOFT TISSUES: No aggressive osseous lesions. LOWER CHEST: Lung bases clear. Mild gynecomastia.
\n

[IMPRESSION]

IMPRESSION:
\n

IMPRESSION:
\n1. Operative changes of segment 5/6 resection followed by Y-90 radioembolization of the right hepatic lobe with progressive volume loss and fibrosis of the 12th thoracic vertebra.
\n2. No new lesions suspicious for hepatocellular carcinoma.
